title: betula alba f. occidentalis
intro: Betula alba f. occidentalis, also known as the Western White Birch, is a deciduous tree species native to the western regions of North America. The bark of this plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Betula alba f. occidentalis are believed to offer health benefits such as soothing skin irritations and reducing fever, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Herbal preparations of the bark can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, wounds, and respiratory problems.
